danger_signals.visual_form: Uniformed infantry and war robots in faction colors
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat; armed opposition forces patrolling territory

danger_signals.visual_form: Sound and visual feedback of gunfire in peaceful areas
danger_signals.meaning: Triggers alarm state; attracts guards and initiates combat

danger_signals.visual_form: Visibly deformed creatures distinct from human soldiers
danger_signals.meaning: Plague victims corrupted by comet-borne virus; unpredictable threats

danger_signals.visual_form: Heavily defended structure with multiple guard positions
danger_signals.meaning: Seat of oppressive power; high-danger military installation

safety_signals.signal: Friendly NPCs in Tarnhill town hub
safety_signals.visual_form: Conversable characters in civilian clothing within settled areas
safety_signals.meaning: Non-hostile; available for dialogue, missions, and trade

safety_signals.visual_form: Secured rebel hideout marked as friendly territory
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zone; allied faction headquarters for rest and mission briefing

safety_signals.visual_form: First aid kits and armor visible in player inventory
safety_signals.meaning: Recovery resources available; player can prepare for danger

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: The Sigil (five-part powerful weapon worshipped by the Order)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Order infantry in uniform; war robots; corrupted mutated servants
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Order castle (fortress stronghold); factory installations; patrol routes in Tarnhill and surrounding areas
Front base hideout; safe houses in Tarnhill town hub; communication network via Blackbird

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Devastated landscape; mutated creatures; religious/cult symbology of The Order; decaying civilization infrastructure
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Survivors enslaved or corrupted; plague victims hearing malevolent deity voice; society reorganized under theocratic control

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Order guards patrol settlements; military installations prominent; castle dominates landscape; civilian areas under surveillance
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs fearful or compliant; guards aggressive toward unauthorized weapons use; restricted movement zones

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Front base established; resistance infrastructure visible; Order fortifications weakened after sabotage missions; castle occupied by Front forces
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Macil commands resistance operations; protagonist receives escalating mission complexity; allied NPCs coordinate attacks

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Each Order leader (Programmer, Bishop, Macil, Oracle) holds fragment; locations change as story progresses
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Protagonist hunts high-value targets; confrontation with Macil's betrayal revealed; moral choice point manifests in NPC behavior

environmental_storytelling.example: Town of Tarnhill serves as central hub allowing non-linear exploration; areas retain state when player leaves, showing progression of resistance efforts and Order decline
Order castle as visual symbol of oppressive power; Front base hideout contrasts with militarized Order installations; environmental design communicates control vs. freedom
Rowan's recruitment offer, Blackbird's radio communication, Macil's command structure, Oracle's revelation—narrative delivered through conversable characters advancing plot and world state
Player begins with dagger; gains crossbow, flamethrower, and eventually Sigil fragments; weapon escalation mirrors protagonist's rising power and deeper involvement in conflict
Mutated enemies distinct from Order soldiers; visual deformity communicates comet-virus consequence; enemy variety signals corrupted vs. organized threats

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Tolerant of unarmed player presence; does not engage
Alarm triggered; guards converge and attack; communication spreads alert to other units

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Stealth attack with dagger or poisoned bolts
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: No alarm triggered; silent elimination possible; stealth mechanics bypass alert system
Available for dialogue; offers missions; provides supplies and information
Assigns sabotage and assault missions; coordinates castle attack; later revealed as traitor with hidden agenda
Provides critical information about Sigil fragments and enemy leader locations; delivers plot twist revealing Macil's betrayal
Mechanized Order enforcer; distinct from human infantry; represents technological oppression of Order regime

notes: Strife innovatively blends FPS combat with RPG dialogue and choice mechanics. The visual communication of world-state relies heavily on NPC interaction, environmental markers of faction control (castle, bases, patrols), and narrative reveals through conversation. The moral choice between trusting Macil or the Oracle represents a pivotal world-state shift where player agency determines factional alignment. Stealth mechanics (dagger, poisoned bolts) allow non-alarm-triggering danger signals, contrasting with overt weapons. The Sigil as MacGuffin visually and narratively organizes the quest structure while embodying The Order's religious/cult ideology.
